data.body:
PASS 1 review — criterion T1 (Prior-art / novel-architecture justification) [overlay]. Target: the "Eval foundation: deterministic-scorer registry + per-reviewer dataset schema (2.1 + 2.3)" plan (relates to this shipped eval-suite ticket; recorded here for traceability, alongside the prior A1/G1G2/G6 PASS-1 reviews of this same plan). Tool-grounded against src/rebar/llm/{eval.py,contracts.py} and src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py.

AFFIRMATIONS (T1 dimensions this plan satisfies — positive feedback, not findings):
- T1(b) novelty justification — NOT APPLICABLE / well-trodden. A name→pure-function registry is an established pattern, not a novel architecture; the "novel architectural pattern" bright-line does not fire. No Rule-of-Three justification is owed (and the pattern is in fact already used >=2x in this repo — see below).
- T1(c) unverified capability assertions — PASS. The plan makes no "library supports X" claims. It builds offline, stdlib-only pure scorers over the ALREADY-SHIPPED Inspect-AI eval seam (src/rebar/llm/eval.py) and the registered contracts (plan_review_findings is registered at src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py:145); no live model calls (AC#5). Every load-bearing capability is verifiable from the repo, not asserted.

FINDING (T1(a) — prior art / anti-repackaging):

- [ ] Name the two existing in-repo registry patterns the proposed scorer registry parallels, and state whether it reuses or deliberately diverges from them (so this is not a third bespoke registry shape re-derived from scratch).
  - criteria: [T1]
  - location: plan "What" §1 ("A name->pure-function registry for deterministic scorers in src/rebar/llm/eval.py (or a new src/rebar/llm/eval_scorers.py)"); plan AC line 1 / AC line 2.
  - evidence:
    - Closest analog ALREADY SHIPS: `rebar.llm.contracts` is a name→callable registry (`_CONTRACTS: dict[str, Callable]`, `register_contract`, `response_model_for`) at src/rebar/llm/contracts.py:24-39 — including the exact "the NAME is the portable handle keyed from YAML because a live class can't live in the DSL" rationale the scorer registry also faces (scorer names come from the eval-spec YAML — plan-review-finder.eval.yaml:19/27). This is the documented reuse seam; the emits_valid_* scorers in particular should DELEGATE to `response_model_for(name)` rather than fork shape-validation (also raised under A1/G6 by the prior reviews on this ticket).
    - Second precedent: `rebar.llm.plan_review.registry` already implements a name-keyed registry WITH a completeness guard — `check_registry_coverage` (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py:300) — directly analogous to this plan's AC2 "an unregistered/typo'd scorer name FAILS the offline gate."
    - ABSENCE: the plan names neither precedent and does not say whether the scorer registry follows contracts.py's register/lookup shape (and whether the unregistered-name gate mirrors check_registry_coverage's guard) or intentionally stands apart. Without naming the prior art, the registry risks being re-derived and drifting from the established idioms.
  - suggested_fix: In "What", add one line stating the registry follows the established `rebar.llm.contracts` name→callable pattern (register + lookup + reject-unknown-name) and that the unregistered-name gate mirrors `plan_review.registry.check_registry_coverage`'s completeness-guard discipline; reuse those idioms rather than introducing a third bespoke registry shape.

Note: coaching / anti-repackaging nudge, not a blocker — the registry choice itself is sound and well-trodden; the ask is only to make the prior-art alignment explicit. This is the sole T1 finding; the chunk is otherwise clean for this pass.
